answers.object: terminal trace widget for lane monitoring
answers.goal: let operators tell quickly when a lane is drifting

answers.object: Web app home surface direction
answers.goal: Lead the web app home surface with linked projects instead of the old idea board.
answers.boundary: This task is limited to the web app's primary home-surface emphasis and navigation between linked projects, the old idea board, and Pensieve.
answers.constraints: Active work should be foregrounded, idea browsing should move into Pensieve, and the old idea board should become secondary navigation.
answers.success_criteria: The web app home surface presents linked projects first, while the old idea board is secondary navigation and idea browsing is routed to Pensieve.

answers.object: Short drift summaries
answers.goal: Test whether short drift summaries help operators notice stalled lanes faster.
answers.success_criteria: Compare stalled-lane pickup with summaries versus without summaries.

answers.object: Kernel handoff experiment
answers.goal: Compare free-form tasks, checklist tasks, and kernel tasks during handoff pickup.
answers.boundary: Scope the experiment to handoff pickup.

answers.object: Habanero routing and runner sync state on active machines
answers.goal: Rerun runner sync on active machines for Habanero.
answers.boundary: This task is limited to active machines that may still need a sync after routing was restored.
answers.success_criteria: Runner sync has been rerun on active machines.

answers.object: Hosted runner pickup routing and job-claim behavior for linked projects and local sessions
answers.goal: Ensure hosted jobs are only claimed for linked projects that have a routeable local session, so claimed work always has a real local execution target.
answers.constraints: Only claim hosted jobs for linked projects with a routeable local session. A claimed job must resolve to an actual local session.
answers.success_criteria: During hosted runner pickup, a job is claimed only if its linked project has a routeable local session, and every claimed job resolves to an actual local session.

answers.object: ORP reasoning kernel release
answers.goal: Run comparative studies against free-form artifacts and checklist artifacts.
answers.boundary: Limit the next work to comparative studies involving the shipped reasoning kernel, free-form artifacts, and checklist artifacts.
answers.success_criteria: Comparative studies have been run against free-form artifacts and checklist artifacts.

answers.object: Build the terminal trace widget for lane monitoring.
answers.goal: Define the event schema and first rendering pass for terminal-first lane visibility in active ORP sessions.
answers.boundary: Terminal-first lane visibility in active ORP sessions.
answers.success_criteria: An operator can identify a drifting lane quickly.

answers.object: Implement the linked-project home shell and lower-right Pensieve access.
answers.goal: Lead with linked projects instead of the old idea-board-first home, while moving broader idea browsing into Pensieve.
answers.constraints: Keep the home calm; preserve access to the full idea library.
answers.success_criteria: Users can reach active work in one click.

answers.object: Design the comparison fixture and timing capture.
answers.goal: Test whether drift summaries improve stalled-lane pickup speed by adding summaries and comparing pickup behavior against a no-summary baseline.
answers.constraints: Operators cannot be flooded with noise.
answers.success_criteria: A comparison fixture and timing capture are defined so stalled-lane detection time can be measured from pickup timing logs for summary and no-summary conditions.

answers.object: Prompt set and reviewer score sheet
answers.goal: Prepare the prompt set and reviewer score sheet for comparing handoff pickup across free-form, checklist, and kernel task artifacts.
answers.constraints: Use a small internal sample and a matched prompt set. Treat the work as a first comparative signal, not a final outcome study.
answers.success_criteria: The prompt set and reviewer score sheet are prepared and support capturing time to correct interpretation and clarification counts.

answers.object: Habanero linked-project routing path
answers.goal: Rerun runner sync on active machines and verify live pickup.
answers.boundary: Habanero runner routing and session availability.
answers.success_criteria: The linked project is routable again, and live pickup is verified after rerunning runner sync on active machines.

answers.object: Pickup behavior for hosted runner jobs on linked projects.
answers.goal: Audit pickup behavior against stale-session and missing-session cases.

answers.object: Comparative artifact and handoff studies for the ORP reasoning kernel
answers.goal: Run comparative artifact and handoff studies next.

answers.object: terminal trace widget
answers.goal: surface lane drift and state clearly for operators
answers.boundary: terminal-first lane visibility; active ORP sessions only
answers.success_criteria: an operator can identify a drifting lane within 10 seconds; the widget does not overload the terminal surface

answers.object: Web app home
answers.goal: Foreground linked projects on the web app home and move broad idea browsing into Pensieve.
answers.boundary: This task is limited to the web app home's primary foreground content and the placement of broad idea browsing in navigation.
answers.constraints: Keep the idea library available as secondary navigation rather than the default home view.
answers.success_criteria: Linked projects are the primary object on the home screen, and broad idea browsing is available one click deeper in Pensieve.

answers.object: Short drift summaries for identifying stalled lanes
answers.goal: Evaluate whether short drift summaries reduce the time needed to identify stalled lanes.
answers.boundary: Terminal-first multi-lane workflows where operators are already monitoring active lanes.
answers.success_criteria: Matched stalled-lane pickup trials with and without summaries are run, and the resulting comparison shows the detection time difference.

answers.object: Kernel task artifacts compared against free-form and generic checklist alternatives in matched handoff trials.
answers.goal: Measure whether kernel task artifacts improve handoff pickup quality over free-form and generic checklist alternatives.
answers.boundary: Run matched handoff trials where a second operator receives one artifact at a time and explains the task, constraints, and next action; interpret the pilot as measuring structural pickup rather than full downstream outcomes.
answers.constraints: Use a matched prompt set, second-operator reviewers, and three artifact conditions; account for the small internal sample size when interpreting results.
answers.success_criteria: Produce pickup scores, clarification counts, and time to correct interpretation, supported by score sheets, timing logs, and an artifact corpus.

answers.object: Habanero runner routing and synchronization across the local project link, primary session, and hosted world.
answers.goal: Rerun runner sync on active machines and verify one fresh hosted job pickup.
answers.boundary: Work is scoped to active machines and the currently synchronized local project link, primary session, and hosted world.
answers.constraints: Inactive machines may still hold stale routing state; older queued jobs may need a fresh sync before pickup.
answers.success_criteria: Runner sync has been rerun on active machines and one fresh hosted job pickup has been verified.

answers.object: Hosted runner job pickup and claim behavior.
answers.goal: Ensure a hosted job is claimed only when the linked project has a routeable local session on the current machine.
answers.boundary: runner sync, poll, and work lifecycle
answers.constraints: Runner routing must remain machine-scoped, and a claimed job must resolve to an actual local session.
answers.success_criteria: Hosted jobs are only claimed when the linked project has a routeable local session on the current machine, every claimed job resolves to an actual local session, and runner routing stays machine-scoped.

answers.object: comparative artifact, pickup, and corpus-fit studies for the ORP reasoning kernel
answers.goal: Run the comparative artifact, pickup, and corpus-fit studies.
answers.boundary: Limit the work to the comparative artifact, pickup, and corpus-fit studies for the shipped ORP reasoning kernel release.
answers.constraints: Do not treat comparative superiority over free-form and checklist alternatives as already proven; account for the fact that current evidence is strongest on internal validity.
answers.success_criteria: The comparative artifact, pickup, and corpus-fit studies are completed in a way that addresses the current interpretation limits.

claims.claim: On the matched live canonical-task continuation benchmark, kernel artifacts produce task artifacts that meet or exceed generic checklist quality without a higher invention rate.
On the matched live canonical-task continuation benchmark, kernel artifacts produce stronger next-task artifacts than free-form artifacts.
On the matched live canonical-task continuation benchmark, kernel artifacts minimize unsupported task-field invention.
